releaseLinks.title: The Adventures of Elliot Release Date, Platforms, and Store Links
releaseLinks.intro: The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales launched on June 18, 2026 for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC, Steam, and Nintendo Switch 2. This section gives players the official places to buy, download, or check the Prologue Demo. The Standard Edition is $59.99 on Steam, PlayStation, and Xbox, with a $69.99 Digital Deluxe Edition and a $10.00 Deluxe Upgrade on supported storefronts.

items.label: Official Website
items.details: Official game hub with overview, trailers, platform links, system specs, screenshots, and demo access.
highlights: HD-2D action RPG from Square Enix and Claytechworks
Lists PlayStation, Xbox, Steam, and Nintendo Switch 2
Hosts trailers, screenshots, and platform specs
Entry point for the Prologue Demo

items.label: Steam
items.details: $59.99 Standard Edition, $69.99 Digital Deluxe Edition, and a $10.00 Deluxe Upgrade.
highlights: Steam Achievements, Steam Cloud, and Family Sharing
Full audio in English and Japanese

items.label: PlayStation
items.details: $59.99 Standard Edition, $69.99 Digital Deluxe Edition, and a $10.00 Deluxe Upgrade.
highlights: PS5 version with Remote Play support

items.label: Xbox
items.details: $59.99+ Standard Edition and $69.99+ Digital Deluxe Edition with a $10.00 Deluxe Upgrade.
highlights: Separate Prologue Demo with Cloud Gaming support

items.label: Nintendo Switch 2
items.details: Regional Nintendo eShop pricing with Digital Deluxe Edition and Deluxe Upgrade options.
highlights: Save Data Cloud via Nintendo Switch Online

reviewRatings.title: The Adventures of Elliot Review Scores, Steam Ratings, and Player Feedback
reviewRatings.intro: Critic reviews praise the HD-2D presentation, brisk action combat, compact pacing, and accessible design. Player storefront data highlights a mostly positive Steam launch response and a strong PlayStation Store rating.

items.detail: 71% positive from 171 user reviews in the Steam All Reviews summary.
items.sentiment: Players are generally positive on PC, with storefront tags emphasizing Action RPG, JRPG, Singleplayer, Co-op, and Local Co-op.
cons: PC experience depends on Windows 11 hardware settings
Review volume still centers on launch-period feedback

items.detail: Average PlayStation Store rating from 1,535 player ratings.
items.sentiment: The PS5 storefront rating is strongly positive and highlights offline 1-2 player support, Remote Play, and DualSense vibration.
pros: High player rating on PlayStation Store
cons: PlayStation language list is narrower than Steam
Storefront review detail is limited compared with critics

items.detail: PC Gamer review by Baxter Burchill, published June 17, 2026.
items.sentiment: A brisk time-traveling action RPG with high-energy hack-and-slash combat, compact pacing, and effective Faie support abilities.

items.detail: GamesRadar+ review by Luke Kemp, published June 17, 2026.
items.sentiment: Expansive without becoming intimidating, varied without becoming overwhelming, and familiar without feeling tired.
cons: Story and writing are less ambitious than the systems
Narrative does not push as hard as the combat design

items.detail: The Guardian review by Tom Regan, published June 18, 2026.
items.sentiment: A charming retro-style action RPG with Zelda-like exploration, treasure hunting, temple roaming, and deeper-than-expected combat customization.
pros: Fast travel and hints improve approachability

prologueDemo.title: The Adventures of Elliot Prologue Demo Guide
prologueDemo.intro: The Prologue Demo lets players experience the opening chapter, explore the early regions, test weapons and Magicite, and carry save data into the full game. Linked save-data bonuses are redeemed in the full release rather than inside the demo.

steps.title: Download the demo from an official platform
steps.description: Use the official Square Enix demo page or the platform store for Steam, PlayStation Store, Xbox Store, or Nintendo Switch 2. The demo is free on listed storefronts.

steps.title: Start with the opening chapter
steps.description: The demo gives access to the early story setup and lets players progress through the main quest while exploring side areas. It shows the same basic freedom players get in the full game.

steps.title: Explore for weapons and Magicite
steps.description: Do not rush only the main path. The demo encourages players to search the field and dungeons for weapons, Magicite, treasure, materials, and useful items.

steps.title: Try every weapon type
steps.description: The PlayStation Blog demo preview says the demo includes all seven weapon types. Players can test close-range and ranged options such as sword, hammer, boomerang, and other styles before choosing preferred combinations.

steps.title: Use Faie for magic, puzzles, and movement
steps.description: Faie is more than a story companion. She can support combat, help solve puzzles, and unlock Warp through the Shrine of the Mystic, letting Elliot teleport to her location.

steps.title: Use fast travel after finding guideposts
steps.description: The demo supports fast travel to discovered adventuring guideposts across the two demo eras, making exploration and cleanup easier after initial discovery.

steps.title: Carry save data into the full game
steps.description: Save data from the Prologue Demo can be transferred to the full game. Linked save-data bonuses are handled in the full release and are not redeemed inside the demo.

steps.title: Know the demo limits
steps.description: The Prologue Demo uses content from the retail game but limits accessible areas. Treat it as a first-chapter preview, early build test, and save-transfer starting point rather than a complete walkthrough.

beginnerGuide.title: The Adventures of Elliot Beginner Guide for Combat, Exploration, and Early Progress
beginnerGuide.intro: The Adventures of Elliot starts like a bright fantasy adventure and then asks players to juggle movement, blocking, weapon durability, Faie control, Magicite choices, hidden loot, and time-travel exploration. The safest early approach is to scout before rushing, block on timing instead of panic-dodging everything, experiment with two equipped weapons, and break suspicious objects for loot.

items.title: Scout ahead with Faie
items.summary: Use Faie to check dangerous paths, attack from a distance, and interact with objects before Elliot walks into trouble.
items.details: Faie can be controlled independently. Send her forward in risky spaces, test enemy positions, grab objects, and help solve puzzles before committing Elliot to a dangerous route.

items.title: Practice perfect blocking early
items.summary: Well-timed blocks reduce damage, can stun enemies, and can reflect certain attacks.
items.details: Blocking is introduced early and is worth practicing immediately. Learn the rhythm of common enemy moves and use timed blocks to create safer counterattack windows.

items.title: Equip two weapons and switch often
items.summary: Elliot can equip up to two weapons at once and swap in real time during combat.
items.details: Use one weapon for safer general fights and another for range, crowd-control, or heavy-hit situations. Weapon switching also helps manage durability and makes early fights smoother.

items.title: Test all seven weapon types in the demo
items.summary: The Prologue Demo includes all seven weapon types, giving players a safe place to find their preferred combat style.
items.details: The PlayStation Blog preview points to the hammer's charge attack and the boomerang's ranged damage. Try every type before judging the combat system, which is built around weapon variety.

items.title: Use Magicite to shape your build
items.summary: Magicite changes weapon behavior, stats, and combat effects.
items.details: Search dungeons and exploration routes for Magicite fragments, then slot them into weapons to strengthen the way you already play. Square Enix's demo feedback also confirms improved Magicite menus and weapon-specific Magicite Boxes.

items.title: Break pots, jars, crates, and grass
items.summary: Small environmental objects can hide materials, loot, and useful items.
items.details: Treat suspicious clutter as a resource check, especially before entering harder areas or spending money. Breaking objects during exploration is a reliable early source of materials.

items.title: Unlock and use fast travel guideposts
items.summary: Discovered adventuring guideposts allow fast travel across demo-era locations.
items.details: Fast travel reduces backtracking after you have opened routes and found guideposts. Prioritize activating guideposts when exploring because later cleanup becomes far easier.

items.title: Visit the Shrine of the Mystic for Faie's Warp
items.summary: Faie's Warp lets Elliot teleport to her position and is useful for both puzzles and enemy pressure.
items.details: Warp is a major early ability learned from a Shrine of the Mystic. Use it for puzzle routing, gap movement, and escaping awkward combat positions.

items.title: Pick a difficulty that matches your goal
items.summary: The final game includes easy, normal, hard, and very hard difficulty settings.
items.details: Square Enix confirmed expanded difficulty options after debut demo feedback. Use easier settings for exploration and story flow, or harder settings when you want blocking and positioning to matter more.

items.title: Do not treat the demo as the full walkthrough
items.summary: The Prologue Demo contains retail content with area limits and save-transfer support.
items.details: Use the demo to learn core systems, collect early gear, test Magicite, and prepare a save file for the full game. Late-game routes and full-era progression belong to the complete release.

weaponBuilds.title: The Adventures of Elliot Weapon Tier List and Magicite Build Ideas
weaponBuilds.intro: Elliot can equip up to two weapons at once and swap between them in real time. Each weapon type has a different combat rhythm, utility use, and Magicite path, so strong builds usually pair one dependable main weapon with one tool weapon for puzzles, range, crowd control, or burst damage.

pairings: Bomb for cracked walls and burst damage
Spear for reach and safer openings

entries.role: Ranged attacks and safe enemy control
mechanics: Special attack fires multiple arrows in a fan
Useful for enemies or switches that are awkward to reach
pairings: Boomerang for item and path utility
Sword and Shield for safe close combat

mechanics: Damages enemies on outbound and return flight
Useful for long-range interaction in exploration
pairings: Sword and Shield for close-range defense
Chains and Sickles for enemy positioning

tiers.label: High value when the encounter fits

entries.role: Reach, forward pressure, safer melee spacing
mechanics: Attacks forward from outside normal melee range
Special attack is a forward charge

entries.role: Heavy hits, pegs, slow high-impact attacks
mechanics: Special attack is a downward blow
pairings: Sword and Shield for faster recovery

entries.role: Range control, surrounding attacks, enemy pull
mechanics: Attacks at range and around Elliot's surrounding area
pairings: Sword and Shield for close-range follow-up

faieGuide.title: The Adventures of Elliot Faie Magic, Warp, and Local Co-op Guide
faieGuide.intro: Faie is Elliot's fairy companion, support partner, and second-player option. Her magic changes how players scout, solve puzzles, move through dungeons, and control enemy encounters. Faie can be controlled directly with the right stick, and a second local player can also control her.

items.title: Control Faie with the Right Stick
details: Faie can move independently from Elliot for scouting and object interaction.
She can retrieve distant objects and inspect dangerous spaces before Elliot moves in.
Useful in dungeons where enemies, switches, hazards, or items sit away from Elliot's direct path.

items.title: Local 1-2 Player Support
details: The game supports local play for one or two players on supported platforms.
One player can control Elliot while another controls Faie.
Faie's independent movement lets the second player support combat, puzzles, and scouting.

items.title: Warp Elliot to Faie
details: Warp is learned from a Shrine of the Mystic.
Warp transports Elliot to Faie's position.
Use it for puzzle movement, avoiding hazards, and repositioning around enemies.

items.title: Light, Melt, and Detonate
details: Ignite can light torches and melt ice.
It can also detonate explosives used in combat and puzzle setups.
Works especially well with bomb-based builds that benefit from controlled explosion timing.

items.title: Pull Enemies and Objects Together
details: Vacuum draws nearby enemies and objects toward Faie's magic.
It can collect dropped items and loot more easily.
Affected enemies can be repositioned for follow-up attacks or moved toward hazards.

items.title: Mirror Elliot's Actions
details: Copy lets Faie mimic Elliot's movement and actions.
It can help with switches and synchronized puzzle actions.
Copy can also interact with sword, spear, bomb, and thrown-item setups.

items.title: Attack, Rescue, and Reposition
details: Faie can help attack enemies from a safer distance.
She can support positioning by setting up Warp locations.
Faie can also revive Elliot by spending tul.

codesBonuses.title: The Adventures of Elliot Codes, Save Data Bonuses, Deluxe Items, and Editions
codesBonuses.intro: The Adventures of Elliot uses platform bonuses, save-data rewards, Digital Deluxe accessories, and Collector's Edition items rather than a general public code list. Use this section to find which rewards come from save data, edition purchases, preorder campaigns, and store bundles.

items.name: OCTOPATH TRAVELER 0 Bonus
items.reward: Sword magicite: Guidance of the Sacred Flame
items.effect: Increases sword damage to enemies with 30% or lower vitality by 10%.
items.claim: Use Promotional Bonus from the title screen, link the eligible title, then claim bonuses from the desk on the second floor of the orphanage in the Age of Safekeeping.

items.name: DRAGON QUEST I & II HD-2D Remake Bonus
items.claim: Claim through the Promotional Bonus flow and the orphanage desk after starting the game.

items.name: BRAVELY DEFAULT FLYING FAIRY HD Remaster Bonus
items.effect: Increases sword damage dealt by 10%, while using a little more shield stamina when guarding.
items.claim: Claim through Promotional Bonus and the orphanage desk reward location.

items.name: VARIOUS DAYLIFE Bonus
items.claim: Claim through the title-screen Promotional Bonus flow and the orphanage desk.

items.name: TRIANGLE STRATEGY Bonus
items.effect: Increases sword damage by 10% while shield stamina is 90% or greater.
items.claim: Claim from the orphanage desk after linking the eligible title from Promotional Bonus.

items.name: Trials of Mana Bonus
items.effect: Ornamental feathers from a certain kingdom; having this item leads to an encounter in town.
items.claim: Claim through the same Promotional Bonus and orphanage desk process.

items.name: Elliot's Departure Pack
items.reward: Departure Brooch accessory and Attack Up sword magicite
items.effect: Departure Brooch increases tul and magicite fragment drops from enemies by a small amount.
items.claim: Included as a digital preorder bonus on participating platform stores before the listed preorder deadline.

items.name: Digital Deluxe Pack
items.reward: Fairy Bangle, Cherry Blossom Anklet, and Roselle Ring
items.effect: Accessory set included with the Digital Deluxe Edition and Digital Deluxe Upgrade.
items.claim: Purchase the Digital Deluxe Edition or the Digital Deluxe Upgrade on supported storefronts.

items.name: Square Enix Store Collector's Edition
items.reward: Physical game copy, Digital Deluxe Upgrade code, original soundtrack, and Faie and Door of Time desktop clock
items.effect: Square Enix Store limited edition package with physical collectibles and digital deluxe content.
items.claim: Purchase through the Square Enix Store while supplies are available.

platformSpecs.title: The Adventures of Elliot System Requirements and Platform Performance
platformSpecs.intro: The Adventures of Elliot is available across modern console and PC platforms with local 1-2 player support listed by official platform materials. PC players should check the minimum and recommended Steam requirements, while console players can compare resolution, frame rate, storage, and platform-specific features before choosing a version.

items.requirements: Windows 11 64-bit, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 12. Min: Ryzen 3 3100 / i3-8100, GTX 1650 @ 720p 60fps. Rec: i5-8600K, GTX 1660 Ti @ 1080p 60fps.
PS5 console. Voice in English and Japanese; screen languages include English, French, German, Italian, Japanese, and Spanish.

features: Cloud Gaming with supported Game Pass tiers
items.requirements: Xbox Series X console. 9 supported languages including Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and Korean text.

features: Digital Deluxe Edition includes base game and upgrade
items.requirements: Xbox Series S console. 9 supported languages listed on Xbox Store.
Windows 11 PC. Connects Xbox and Windows versions through Xbox Play Anywhere.

items.frameRate: TV mode up to 60 fps; handheld variable 30-60 fps
items.requirements: Nintendo Switch 2 console. Languages include Japanese, English, Spanish, French, German, and Italian.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales

questions.question: Is The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales single-player or multiplayer?
questions.answer: The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales supports single-player and local co-op for 1-2 players, with the second player able to control Faie directly during exploration and combat.

questions.question: What platforms is The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales available on?
questions.answer: The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales is available on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, PC via Steam and the Windows Store, and Nintendo Switch 2, all launching on June 18, 2026.

questions.question: How much does The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales cost?
questions.answer: The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales Standard Edition is $59.99, with a $69.99 Digital Deluxe Edition and a $10.00 Deluxe Upgrade available on supported storefronts.

questions.question: Is there a free demo for The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales?
questions.answer: Yes. The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales Prologue Demo is free on Steam, PlayStation Store, Xbox Store, and Nintendo Switch 2, and its save data carries over into the full game.

questions.question: What is Faie in The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales?
questions.answer: Faie is Elliot's fairy companion in The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales. She scouts ahead, retrieves items, supports combat, and learns magic such as Warp, Ignite, Vacuum, and Copy from Shrines of the Mystic.

questions.question: How many weapon types are in The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales?
questions.answer: The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales has seven weapon types, including the Sword and Shield, Bow, Bomb, Boomerang, Spear, Hammer, and Chains and Sickles, with two equipped at once and swappable in real time.

cta.title: Ready to Explore The Adventures of Elliot?
cta.description: An HD-2D action RPG across four ages. Play the demo or wishlist on Steam, and follow the official channels for the latest updates.
